text: First time storing data with Python and JSON. I wrote a small script that reads and writes reflections.json, which gave me a simple, file-based way to keep entries without setting up a full database.

Moved the backend from Python's built-in http.server to Flask. Routes like /api/reflections are much clearer than parsing the request path by hand, and returning data with jsonify() keeps everything tidy.

The service worker took a few attempts before offline mode actually worked. Versioning the cache name and deleting old caches on activate was the part I had missed, and once fixed the pages loaded with no connection.

My earlier version only worked on my laptop because the fetch calls pointed at http://localhost:8000. Switching to relative URLs like /api/reflections meant the exact same code ran on PythonAnywhere over HTTPS.
